mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal zero lower bound monetary policy, reducing discount rates on equity valuations and increasing forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through lower risk-free rates compressing equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.05% on 2020-04-01 indicates emergency monetary policy
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 10% signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering equity market repricing downward as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases. Forward S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days as market participants process labor shock.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 14.7% on 2020-04-01 reflects COVID-19 labor shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 10%, or the negative direction signal fails to predict lower returns relative to baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 5% signals demand destruction and consumer retrenchment, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 403,946M on 2020-04-01 reflects pandemic demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines > 5%, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 85 (index 2017=100) while unemployment rises simultaneously, indicating both supply-side collapse and demand weakness. This joint deterioration signals stagflationary pressure that equity markets reprrice negatively over 60 trading days as profit margins compress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.59 on 2020-04-01, UNRATE at 14.7% indicates dual shock
regime_hint: stagflationary shock with supply and demand collapse
failure_condition: The interaction effect (INDPRO × UNRATE) produces Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day returns are not significantly lower when both conditions are met versus when only one is met (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) recover month-over-month by more than 10% after a decline, signaling renewed construction demand and consumer confidence. This recovery mechanism drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through multiplier effects on employment and materials demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 10% month-over-month following a prior decline
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,216K on 2020-03-01 shows pandemic-era volatility
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST rebounds > 10%, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward Sharpe ratio tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating accelerating labor market deterioration. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings and growth expectations downward in response to employment shock.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,045K on 2020-04-01, UNRATE at 14.7% reflects coordinated labor shock
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS decline × UNRATE rise) fails to predict negative 5-day returns with Rank IC > 0.02, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate is at zero lower bound, signaling deflationary pressure and justifying continued monetary accommodation. This regime supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through sustained low discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 255.90 on 2020-04-01, FEDFUNDS at 0.05% indicates deflationary ZLB regime
regime_hint: deflationary zero lower bound monetary regime
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.025, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) contracts quarter-over-quarter (negative growth rate) while retail sales decline simultaneously, signaling synchronized demand collapse. This dual contraction predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate downward and recession risk crystallizes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate < 0% AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,987.88B on 2020-01-01 (pre-pandemic), RSAFS at 403,946M on 2020-04-01 reflects demand shock
failure_condition: The dual contraction signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day returns are not significantly lower when both conditions are met versus baseline (t-test p > 0.10).
